channel.title: Frontion News Headlines
channel.description: Global geopolitical news curated by Frontion

item.title: 🇧🇾🇺🇦 Zelensky Warns Lukashenko Again as Russia Pushes Belarus to Join War
item.description: Ukraines president revealed that Russia is trying to persuade Belarus to join new aggressive actions against Ukraine, warning Lukashenko about the consequences.

item.title: 🇹🇼🇺🇸 Trump Warns Taiwan: No Blank Check from US Military
item.description: President Trump said Taiwan should not expect a blank check from the US military and stated he is not looking to have someone go independent, in comments about the Taiwan question.

item.title: ⚔️🇷🇺 Sarmat ICBM Has No Analogues, Can Penetrate Any Defense System
item.description: Russian Deputy Foreign Minister Sergey Ryabkov stated that the Sarmat missile system can penetrate both existing and any missile defense systems still in development.

item.title: 🇸🇾 Kurdish Groups Reject Syrias Allocation of Only 4 Parliament Seats
item.description: Syrian Kurdish groups rejected the allocation of only four seats for Kurds in Syrias 210-member parliament, calling the representation inadequate.

item.title: 🇳🇬⚔️ Nigeria Confirms Killing of Senior ISIS Leader in Joint US Operation
item.description: Nigerias President Tinubu and military confirmed the killing of ISIS second-in-command Abu-Bilal al-Minuki in Nigeria during a joint US-Nigeria operation.

item.title: 🇬🇧📉 UK Markets Slide on Political Turmoil and Oil Price Fears
item.description: British equities and bonds fell as investors weighed a potential leadership challenge to PM Keir Starmer alongside mounting concerns about oil-driven inflation from the Iran-Hormuz crisis.

item.title: 🥩🇺🇸🇨🇳 China Renews Export Licenses for 400+ US Beef Plants After Trump-Xi Summit
item.description: China renewed export licenses that had expired for more than 400 U.S. beef plants, the Chinese customs website showed, a potential sign of thawing trade relations following the Beijing summit between Presidents Trump and Xi.

item.title: 🇨🇳🇺🇸🇮🇷 Trump Says Xi Agrees Iran Must Open Hormuz Strait, China Says War Should Not Have Started
item.description: U.S. President Donald Trump said Chinese President Xi Jinping agreed Tehran must reopen the Strait of Hormuz after their Beijing summit, though China gave no indication it would intervene militarily in the conflict.

item.title: 🛢️📉 Iraq Oil Exports Through Hormuz Drop 90% as Strait Blockade Bites
item.description: Iraq exported just 10 million barrels of oil through the Strait of Hormuz in April, down from about 93 million barrels monthly before the Iran conflict began, the country's new oil minister said, highlighting the severe impact on global energy flows.

item.title: 📈💰 US Treasury Yields Hit One-Year High as Oil Prices and Inflation Rattle Markets
item.description: U.S. Treasury yields climbed to their highest levels in a year as a spike in oil prices tied to Middle East energy disruptions, inflation concerns and expectations for a stronger economy led investors to reevaluate interest rate trajectories.

item.title: 🏦🇨🇳 Chinese Officials Meet Citigroup and Goldman Sachs CEOs in Beijing
item.description: China's securities regulator chairman and Beijing's party secretary held talks with Citigroup CEO Jane Fraser, discussing enhanced cooperation in wealth management and cross-border financing following the Trump-Xi summit.

item.title: 🇨🇩🦠 Ebola Outbreak in Eastern Congo Kills 80, US CDC Monitoring
item.description: A new Ebola outbreak in the Democratic Republic of Congo's Ituri province has killed at least 80 people, with 246 suspected cases identified. The US CDC is monitoring the situation and providing technical assistance to DRC and Uganda.

item.title: 🇮🇱🇱🇧🕊️ Israel and Lebanon Extend Ceasefire by 45 Days After US-Brokered Talks
item.description: Israel and Lebanon agreed to extend their ceasefire by 45 days after Washington-mediated negotiations, with further meetings planned in coming weeks. The ceasefire has reduced conflict between Israel and Hezbollah in southern Lebanon.

item.title: 🇮🇷⚖️ Iran FM Says Trust Is Main Obstacle in Nuclear Talks With US
item.description: Iran's foreign minister Abbas Araghchi said a lack of trust is the biggest obstacle in negotiations to end the war with the US, expressing openness to diplomatic help from China. He acknowledged that enriched uranium discussions have hit a deadlock.

item.title: 🇺🇸🇮🇷📉 Analysis: Trump's Geopolitical Brinkmanship Hits Wall With Iran
item.description: Reuters analysis examines how President Trump's blustery negotiating style, which won concessions on other issues, has faced significant challenges with Iran amid ongoing tensions over nuclear weapons and the Strait of Hormuz.

item.title: 🇪🇸🎤 Spain Boycotts Eurovision Over Israel Participation
item.description: Spain's public broadcaster withdrew from the Eurovision Song Contest, protesting Israel's participation over its war against Hamas in Gaza. The boycott marks the first time in seven years Spain will not participate.

item.title: 🇮🇷 Trump Accepts 20-Year Iran Nuclear Freeze as Sufficient for Deal
item.description: President Trump indicated he would accept a 20-year suspension of Iran nuclear program, a shift from previous demands for permanent cessation. The comments came after his summit with Xi in Beijing, with both sides still far apart on key issues.

item.title: 🇳🇬 US and Nigerian Forces Kill Senior ISIS Commander
item.description: President Trump announced that US and Nigerian military forces killed Abu-Bilal al-Minuki, described as the second-in-command of ISIS globally and "the most active terrorist in the world," in a joint operation in Nigeria.

item.title: ✈️ Boeing Secures 200-Plane Order From China After Trump-Xi Summit
item.description: Boeing confirmed its first major sale to China in nearly a decade with an order for 200 aircraft. Trump said China reserved the right to purchase up to 750 planes, marking a breakthrough for the aerospace giant.

item.title: 🇰🇿🇹🇷 Kazakhstan and Turkey Deepen Strategic Ties on Middle Corridor Route
item.description: Presidents Tokayev and Erdogan signed a Declaration on Eternal Friendship and Expanded Strategic Partnership, pledging to raise bilateral trade to $15 billion and expand the Trans-Caspian International Transport Route as a key alternative trade corridor amid regional instability.

item.title: 🎯 Chinese Defense Firms Claim New Sniper Rifle Range Record
item.description: Two Chinese defense companies announced they may have broken a long-distance shooting record, following last year confirmation of hitting five targets at 3,017 meters with the domestically developed CS/LR24 rifle using 8.6mm rounds.

item.title: 🇨🇳🇩🇪 Chinese Investor Acquires 120-Year-Old German Textile Machinery Maker
item.description: Family-owned Mayer & Cie, a German circular knitting machine manufacturer with over 80,000 machines worldwide serving brands like H&M and Uniqlo, has been acquired by Chinese investor Huixing amid industry pressure from Chinese competitors.

item.title: 🇺🇦 Russia and Ukraine exchange 205 prisoners each in latest swap
item.description: The prisoner exchange marks a continued diplomatic effort despite ongoing hostilities, with both sides bringing home service personnel from Russian and Ukrainian captivity.

item.title: 🇷🇺 Zelenskiy warns Russia may be planning NATO attack from Belarus
item.description: Ukraine's president says intelligence suggests Russia is considering military operations against a NATO country using Belarusian territory, raising concerns about escalation.

item.title: 🇸🇩 Drone warfare becomes deadliest threat to Sudanese civilians
item.description: Armed drones account for over 80% of conflict-related civilian deaths in Sudan, with at least 880 civilians killed by drone strikes between January and April 2026.
